title: delphinium linearilobum var. crispulum
intro: Delphinium linearilobum var. crispulum, also known as the Alpine Larkspur, is a perennial plant belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its unique, crinkled or ruffled foliage and delicate, blue or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Alpine Larkspur are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce anxiety. Alpine Larkspur is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, salves, and poultices.
